description: Acquired Black Creek Group, a Canadian real estate and private equity manager with ~$15B AUM, in Q1 2022 to expand real assets platform and gain access to non-traded REIT distribution
stated_rationale: Strengthen real assets vertical and establish non-traded vehicle distribution capabilities for retail investors
observable_metric_impact: Real Assets AUM grew from ~$35B to ~$50B; added non-traded REIT (AREIT) to product suite
op_prereq_summary: Integration of Black Creek investment teams and fund administration into Ares Real Assets group; migration of non-traded vehicle servicing and compliance infrastructure

description: Launched Aspida Financial insurance platform in 2024, acquiring a block of fixed annuity liabilities to create a captive insurance capital channel similar to Apollo/Athene and KKR/Global Atlantic models
stated_rationale: Establish insurance-linked permanent capital to diversify funding sources and grow fixed income origination capacity
observable_metric_impact: Aspida contributed incremental AUM and provided permanent capital base for credit origination; insurance AUM targeted to grow to $50B+
op_prereq_summary: Required insurance regulatory licensing, dedicated insurance investment management team, asset-liability matching infrastructure, and state insurance department approvals

description: Expanded European direct lending franchise significantly from 2022-2025, establishing Ares as the largest non-bank direct lender in Europe with dedicated European Direct Lending funds (EDL IV, V)
stated_rationale: Capture post-Basel III/IV banking retrenchment in European middle-market lending; leverage regulatory-driven opportunity for non-bank credit providers
observable_metric_impact: European credit AUM grew substantially; EDL IV raised over EUR 10B; FRE margin in Credit group expanded to ~45%
op_prereq_summary: Required expansion of London and European offices, local credit underwriting teams, European fund administration infrastructure, and AIFMD compliance across multiple jurisdictions

description: Scaled PE secondaries platform post-Landmark Partners acquisition (2021), launching Landmark Equity Partners XVIII and growing secondaries AUM to ~$30B by 2025
stated_rationale: Leverage Landmark 30-year secondaries track record to build a third major pillar alongside credit and real assets
observable_metric_impact: Secondaries AUM grew from ~$18B at acquisition to ~$30B; provided diversification of PE revenue away from primary buyout
op_prereq_summary: Integration of Landmark Partners team and fund structures into Ares PE group; unified LP reporting and back-office systems; cross-selling to existing Ares LP base
